name: cli
context: Supabase CLI — init projects, run local dev, manage migrations, deploy edge functions, generate types
info: The Supabase CLI (supabase) is the primary tool for local development, schema migrations, edge function deployment, and type generation. It orchestrates Docker containers for a full local Supabase stack (Postgres, GoTrue, PostgREST, Studio, Realtime, Storage). Key workflows: `supabase init` scaffolds a project, `supabase start` launches local services, `supabase db diff` generates migrations from schema changes, `supabase db push` applies migrations to remote, `supabase functions deploy` ships edge functions, and `supabase gen types` produces TypeScript definitions from your schema. Authentication uses `supabase login` with a personal access token. The CLI also manages secrets, links to remote projects, and runs database resets for clean testing.
body:
# Supabase CLI

The Supabase CLI manages the entire development lifecycle: project scaffolding, local development with Docker, database migrations, edge function deployment, and TypeScript type generation.

## Installation

```bash
# npm (recommended)
npm install -g supabase

# Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew install supabase/tap/supabase

# npx (no install)
npx supabase <command>
```

## Authentication

```bash
# Login with access token (interactive)
supabase login

# Login with token directly (CI/CD)
export SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=sbp_abc123...
```

Generate tokens at https://supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens.

## Project Setup

```bash
# Initialize a new Supabase project (creates supabase/ directory)
supabase init

# Link to an existing remote project
supabase link --project-ref <project-id>

# Start local development stack (Docker required)
supabase start

# Check status of local services
supabase status

# Stop local services
supabase stop
```

`supabase start` launches Docker containers and prints local URLs:
```
API URL: http://localhost:54321
GraphQL URL: http://localhost:54321/graphql/v1
DB URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/postgres
Studio URL: http://localhost:54323
anon key: eyJhbGciOi...
service_role key: eyJhbGciOi...
```

## Database Migrations

See [[supabase/migrations]] for full migration workflow.

```bash
# Create a new migration file
supabase migration new create_users_table

# Auto-generate migration from local schema diff
supabase db diff --use-migra -f create_users_table

# Apply migrations to remote database
supabase db push

# Reset local database (drops and recreates from migrations + seed)
supabase db reset

# Pull remote schema into local migrations
supabase db pull
```

## Type Generation

```bash
# Generate TypeScript types from remote database
supabase gen types typescript --project-id <ref> > src/types/supabase.ts

# Generate from local database
supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/supabase.ts

# Generate from linked project
supabase gen types typescript --linked > src/types/supabase.ts
```

Usage with the client:
```typescript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
import type { Database } from './types/supabase';

const supabase = createClient<Database>(
  process.env.SUPABASE_URL!,
  process.env.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
);

// Now fully typed!
const { data } = await supabase.from('users').select('id, email');
// data is { id: string; email: string }[] | null
```

## Edge Functions

See [[supabase/edge-functions]] for full edge function workflow.

```bash
# Create a new edge function
supabase functions new hello-world

# Serve locally for development
supabase functions serve

# Deploy a single function
supabase functions deploy hello-world

# Deploy all functions
supabase functions deploy

# Set secrets for edge functions
supabase secrets set MY_SECRET=value123
supabase secrets list
```

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Inspect remote project status
supabase inspect db table-sizes --linked
supabase inspect db index-usage --linked
supabase inspect db cache-hit --linked

# Seed the local database
supabase db reset  # Runs migrations + supabase/seed.sql

# Dump remote database
supabase db dump -f dump.sql --linked
```

## Configuration (config.toml)

The `supabase/config.toml` file configures local development:

```toml
[project]
id = "your-project-ref"

[api]
enabled = true
port = 54321

[db]
port = 54322
major_version = 15

[studio]
enabled = true
port = 54323

[auth]
enabled = true
site_url = "http://localhost:3000"

[auth.external.github]
enabled = true
client_id = "env(GITHUB_CLIENT_ID)"
secret = "env(GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET)"
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN` | CLI authentication token |
| `SUPABASE_DB_URL` | Direct Postgres connection (for `db push` with `--db-url`) |

sections.title: Installation
sections.body: Install globally via `npm install -g supabase` or `brew install supabase/tap/supabase`. For one-off use, `npx supabase <command>` works without installation. The CLI requires Docker for local development (`supabase start`). Verify with `supabase --version`.

sections.title: Local development workflow
sections.body: `supabase init` creates the `supabase/` directory with `config.toml`. `supabase start` launches Docker containers for Postgres, GoTrue, PostgREST, Studio, Realtime, and Storage. Studio runs at `http://localhost:54323` for visual database management. `supabase stop` shuts everything down. `supabase status` prints all local URLs and keys. The local anon key and service role key are stable across restarts.

sections.title: TypeScript type generation
sections.body: `supabase gen types typescript` introspects your Postgres schema and generates a `Database` type with full table, view, and function signatures. Pass `--local` for local DB, `--linked` for remote, or `--project-id <ref>` for a specific project. Pipe output to a file: `supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/supabase.ts`. Pass the `Database` generic to `createClient<Database>()` for fully typed queries. Re-run after every migration.

sections.title: Configuration (config.toml)
sections.body: The `supabase/config.toml` file configures local services: API port, DB port and version, Studio settings, auth providers, SMTP for email, and edge function settings. Use `env(VAR_NAME)` syntax to reference environment variables for secrets like OAuth client IDs. The `[auth.external.<provider>]` sections configure OAuth providers for local development.
